title: Artemis 2 astronauts get the star treatment after historic moon trip
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts received significant media attention following their historic moon mission announcement. Despite not having a parade, they were prominently featured on major news networks.

title: Drone radar could help spacecraft pinpoint where to drill for water on Mars, scientists say
summary: Scientists propose using drone-mounted radar to map buried ice on Mars, aiding future spacecraft in pinpointing drilling locations. The technology was successfully tested on Earth's glaciers.

title: 'Slither' at 20: The alien worm comedy-horror that heralded James Gunn's arrival
summary: The article discusses the 2006 film 'Slither', a comedy-horror movie that marked James Gunn's directorial debut. Despite limited theater attendance, the film showcased Gunn's potential as a filmmaker.

title: £115 off for Star Wars Day, I think this Lego Star Wars UCS set is the most mind-blowing Lego starship you'll ever own
summary: Argos UK is offering a 20% discount on the Lego Star Wars Clone Wars Venator starship set for Star Wars Day. The massive 5,000+ piece UCS set is highlighted as the best Lego starship available.

title: Thinner than a hair and stretchy like rubber: New material could shield against radiation in next-gen space tech
summary: Scientists developed a thin, stretchy material that could shield against radiation in next-gen space tech. This innovation could protect humans and critical technology during space missions.

title: Is it cake? No, it's a parachute! | Space photo of the day for May 1, 2026
summary: A donut-shaped bag containing a massive parachute is featured in a space photo for May 1, 2026, hinting at its use for Mars missions.

title: The Claypool Lennon Delirium's cosmic new album is not just an AI warning, but a reflection on a global loss of empathy (video)
summary: The Claypool Lennon Delirium's new album 'The Great Parrot-Ox and the Golden Egg of Empathy' explores themes of AI, art, and a global decline in empathy. The band members discuss these concepts in a recent video interview.

title: June full moon 2026: When, where and how to see the Strawberry Moon
summary: The news discusses the June full moon in 2026, known as the Strawberry Moon, including when and where to see it, along with skywatching highlights.

title: NASA's Curiosity and Perseverance rovers capture sweeping Mars panoramas (video)
summary: NASA's Curiosity and Perseverance rovers have captured new panoramic views of Mars, showcasing diverse terrains shaped by ancient water and geological processes. The images highlight the planet's dramatic landscapes and evolutionary history.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its Starship Flight 12 mission, featuring a revised trajectory. The upcoming flight aims to build on previous test objectives with improved performance.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday, marking a significant milestone for the country's space program. The debut flight signifies a major advancement in Russia's launch capabilities.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The astronaut has experience in mass casualty events and overseas service.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return to flight for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center, following the historic Artemis II lunar flyby mission. This marks a key step toward the next crewed Moon landing.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: Falcon Heavy, Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites this week from multiple locations. SpaceX's Falcon Heavy is making a return after a period of inactivity.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is set to launch from Baikonur to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the CRS NG-24 mission two weeks prior.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the Starship's Block 3 variant at the Cape, aiming for a launch this year. The first flight of Block 3 is anticipated as the company advances toward this goal.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the development of next-generation spacesuits. The report raises concerns about the timeline and funding for the program.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over the past 50 years. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute system underwent a critical test known as the 'great parachute bake-out' to ensure its reliability in extreme conditions. This test is likely part of preparations for a future Mars landing or high-altitude mission.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session in April-May and an in-person event on 8-9 June 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris. The forum aims to gather feedback on topics affecting the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement has been highlighted as ideal for navigation purposes.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Space Nuclear Execs Cheer the FY27 Budget Proposal
summary: Space nuclear executives are enthusiastic about NASA's FY27 budget proposal, which NASA Administrator Isaacman views as a 'logical evolution' for the agency.

title: House Subcommittee Approves $24.4B NASA Budget
summary: A House subcommittee has approved a $24.4 billion NASA budget, rejecting the administration's FY27 request. Lawmakers expressed dissatisfaction with the administration's proposed spending plan for the agency.

title: European Space Firms Raised €1.4B of Private Capital in 2025
summary: European space companies secured €1.4 billion in private funding in 2025, reflecting growing investor confidence in the sector. This surge highlights the expanding role of private capital in Europe's space industry.

title: BioOrbit Raises £9.8M Seed To Manufacture Cancer Drugs in Orbit
summary: BioOrbit has raised £9.8 million in seed funding to manufacture cancer drugs in orbit, leveraging microgravity to reformulate drugs for self-administration by patients.

title: CATALYST Unveils its Sat Product for City Skylines
summary: CATALYST has introduced a new satellite product designed for urban planning and construction monitoring. The tool aims to provide insights into pre-construction activity, risk assessment for insurers, and consequences of urban densification for city authorities.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire ALL.SPACE for $355M
summary: York Space Systems is set to acquire ALL.SPACE for $355 million, aiming to expand into high-growth markets like communications and multi-domain military applications.

title: Orbital Data Center Competitive Landscape
summary: The competitive landscape for orbital data centers is being shaped by major players like SpaceX, Blue Origin, and Sophia Space, along with satellite manufacturers such as Apex and K2 Space.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: DPhi Space successfully demonstrated in-orbit AI processing by running Liquid AI's large language model on an Earth observation image without ground transmission. The achievement, part of the Clustergate-2 mission launched on Momentus' Vigoride 7 satellite, marks a new era in space-based AI computing.

title: Golden Dome Will Use COTS Parts, Space Execs Say
summary: Golden Dome satellite bus will utilize Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) parts, according to space executives. Apex Space CEO Ian Cinnamon emphasized its standard commercial design with no unique features.

title: Dems Slam Trump Over NSF Board Cuts
summary: Democrats criticized former President Trump for proposed cuts to the National Science Foundation (NSF) board, calling it detrimental to science and innovation. The statement was made by Rep. Zoe Lofgren (D-CA) in response to the policy change.

title: Loft to Build 10 EO Satellites for French Constellation With Magellium Artal Group
summary: Loft will build 10 Earth Observation (EO) satellites for a French constellation in partnership with Magellium Artal Group. This collaboration marks a significant step in France's next-generation EO satellite initiatives.

title: Orbital Warfare Systems Top Priority for Space Chief Nominee Lt. Gen. Schiess
summary: The White House's nominee to succeed Gen. B. Chance Saltzman prioritizes orbital warfare systems. Lt. Gen. Schiess Fielding emphasizes the need for more such systems.

title: Sateliot and Telefónica España to Explore 5G Satellite Connectivity for Defense, Military Uses
summary: Sateliot and Telefónica España have partnered to explore 5G satellite connectivity for defense, military, and remote area applications using Sateliot's satellite IoT technology.

title: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani Talks Network Resiliency at Modern Day Marine
summary: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani discussed tactical, multi-orbit connectivity solutions for network resiliency at Modern Day Marine. The conversation highlighted Lite Coms' product designs aimed at enhancing communication resilience.

title: Skynopy Signs Frequency Management Partnership with CNES
summary: Skynopy has partnered with CNES to optimize frequency allocation for its ground stations in France. The collaboration aims to enhance frequency management efficiency.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Terminal Developer All.Space in $355M Deal
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring terminal developer All.Space in a $355 million deal to create a complete communications ecosystem. The acquisition aims to strengthen York's position in the space communications sector.

title: FCC Updates EPFD Framework that Covers GEO/NGSO Spectrum Sharing
summary: The FCC adopted a new framework for Equivalent Power Flux Density (EPFD) to govern spectrum-sharing between GEO and NGSO systems. This update addresses longstanding concerns about interference and coordination in satellite communications.

title: EarthDaily CEO Don Osborne Previews Upcoming Launch in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: EarthDaily Analytics is preparing to launch six satellites this quarter, marking a significant milestone as it transitions to commercial service. CEO Don Osborne discussed the upcoming launch in a recent conversation.

title: Department of Air Force Plans Initiative to Hire AI Talent
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force is launching an initiative to recruit top AI talent, which will impact the government's space strategy. This move aims to enhance technological capabilities in the space sector.

title: Canada Cancels WildFireSat Constellation Contract With Spire
summary: Canada has canceled its contract with Spire Global to build the WildFireSat constellation for wildfire monitoring. The government remains committed to the space-based wildfire monitoring program despite the cancellation.
